content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-6

## Summary

Thorough review of the planned-elsewhere reconciliation feature. The implementation is well-structured with clear separation of concerns: data model in milestone_record/model.rs, persistence in fs.rs and service.rs, engine-time recording in engine.rs, and post-run verification/commenting in success_reconciliation.rs and planned_elsewhere.rs. The crash-safety design is sound — journal is authoritative, NDJSON sidecar is write-through, and the load path rebuilds from journal events with identity-based collapse. The verify-then-persist-then-comment phasing in success_reconciliation prevents duplicate comments on replay. Conservative merge semantics for conflicting mapped_to_bead_id during normalization is correct. The reconstruct_missing_pe_mappings fallback from aggregate artifacts covers the engine-failure window. Test coverage is comprehensive across all acceptance criteria: verified bead, stale bead fallback, closed bead, comment posting, persistence survival, transient error propagation, and journal rebuild. No bugs, safety issues, or correctness gaps found.

## Amendments

None.
